userMessage: Extract all invoice line items from this restaurant invoice table image.

instructions.text: Extract ALL invoice line items visible in the table.
instructions.risk: Enables over-extraction; no row-count ceiling or artigo-code anchor

instructions.text: quantity must represent the PURCHASED quantity. unit must represent the PURCHASED unit.
instructions.risk: Ambiguous when description pack spec vs column disagree

instructions.text: NEVER invent values. But DO infer quantity/unit when clearly present inside product names. Screenshots may have messy OCR. Use contextual reasoning.
instructions.risk: CRITICAL — direct contradiction; 'clearly present' undefined; contextual reasoning on messy OCR invites guessing

instructions.text: "Acém Novilho Extra s/ osso 15kg" → quantity: 15, unit: "kg"
instructions.risk: Teaches extracting embedded weight as purchased qty when column may differ

instructions.text: "Bacon Burger Premium Fatiado 1kg" → quantity: 1, unit: "kg"
instructions.risk: Same pattern — 1kg in name becomes qty=1

instructions.text: "Peito de Frango Calibrado" with visible quantity column "10" → quantity: 10, unit: "kg"
instructions.risk: Unit inferred from product type (kg) not column — mixed column/name authority

instructions.text: "Coca-Cola 33cl Pack 24" → quantity: 24, unit: "un"
instructions.risk: HIGH — teaches *N pack count as purchased qty; correct for Birra Peroni, wrong for CX 2.5KG*6

instructions.text: "Água 25cl Pack 24" → quantity: 24, unit: "un"
instructions.risk: Reinforces pack-count-as-qty pattern

instructions.text: "Hamburger Angus 180gr Caixa 40 un" → quantity: 40, unit: "un"
instructions.risk: Teaches ignoring per-item weight (180gr) in favor of case count

instructions.text: "Pão Brioche 80g 120 un" → quantity: 120, unit: "un"
instructions.risk: Same case-count pattern

instructions.text: If product size exists separately from purchased quantity: purchased quantity = pack/case/unit count, NOT the per-item weight
instructions.risk: Correct intent but examples only cover retail bottle/case; no wholesale CX*N case notation

instructions.text: BAD: 180g / GOOD: 40 un
instructions.risk: Binary heuristic; no rule for CX 2.5KG*6 where *6 is units-per-case not purchased qty

instructions.text: BAD: 33cl / GOOD: 24 un
instructions.risk: Reinforces bottle-count extraction — directly applicable to Ginger Beer error

instructions.text: Use these normalized units only when possible: kg, g, L, ml, un, cx, mo, em
instructions.risk: LOW — may coerce visible column units

instructions.text: MO/maço → mo; EM/embalagem → em (preserve exactly — do NOT coerce to cx)
instructions.risk: LOW — supplier-specific; not implicated in VL errors

instructions.text: "Tomilho" with quantity column "1" and unit "MO" → quantity: 1, unit: "mo"
instructions.risk: LOW — column-faithful example

instructions.text: "Manjericão" with unit "MO" → unit: "mo"
instructions.risk: MEDIUM — unit inferred from name/context without qty; encourages partial fill

instructions.text: "Salada" with unit "EM" → quantity: 1, unit: "em"
instructions.risk: MEDIUM — implicit qty=1 when not visible in column

instructions.text: If quantity truly cannot be determined: quantity = null, unit = null
instructions.risk: Contradicted by infer-from-name rule; GPT rarely emits null per audits

instructions.text: Do not hallucinate.
instructions.risk: Weak guard — no definition of hallucination; contradicted by infer + extract-all

instructions.text: Do NOT extract supplier, total, invoice_date, or invoice_number.
instructions.risk: LOW — field-scope guard; does not prevent phantom line items

instructions.text: Read quantity, PREÇO UNITÁRIO, and VALOR DA MERCADORIA digit by digit from their respective columns.
instructions.risk: MEDIUM — column-faithful intent; undermined by infer-from-name for qty

instructions.text: Do not confuse 8 and 9 (e.g. 9,99 misread as 8,99 or 9,49).
instructions.risk: LOW — awareness only; Prosciutto 8.17→17.06 still occurs

instructions.text: quantity, unit_price, and total are each authoritative — copy each exactly from the invoice.
instructions.risk: CRITICAL contradiction with infer-from-name (lines 33-34, 40-67)

instructions.text: quantity × unit_price may NOT equal total. Never alter quantity or unit_price to force arithmetic closure.
instructions.risk: MEDIUM — allows semantically wrong triples (Ginger Beer 24×€0.85=€19.38)

instructions.text: When quantity is 1, unit_price usually equals the line total column (unless a line discount applies).
instructions.risk: LOW — heuristic for single-pack lines

instructions.text: Extract all invoice line items from this restaurant invoice table image.
instructions.risk: Reinforces extract-all without precision guard

absentGuards: No rule distinguishing pack metadata (*N in CX 2.5KG*6) from purchased quantity column
No rejection of lot numbers (Lote, Nº, Data Exp) as standalone line items
No instruction to prefer column qty over description *N when both present
